When Israel was under pressure, they would claim protection from God, but this would be ignored. What use is it to say, ‘My God, I know you,’ if we cannot say, ‘My God, I love you, serve you, and remain faithful to you alone’?

They promised themselves plenty, peace, and victory by worshipping idols, but their expectations amounted to nothing. What they sow produces no grain, no stalk, or if it does, the bud will yield no fruit; there was nothing in them. The works of darkness are unproductive; indeed, the end of those things is death. The hopes of sinners will deceive them, and their gains will be traps. In times of danger, especially in the day of judgment, all worldly plans will fail. They go their own way, and like a wild ass by itself, they will be easier and surer prey for the lion. Man is no more like the wild ass’s colt than in seeking help and satisfaction from creation, which are to be found in God alone. Though people may grieve a little, if it is not according to God's way, they will be brought to eternal sorrow.

It is a great sin to corrupt the worship of God, and it will be charged as sin on all who do it, no matter how reasonable their excuses seem to be. The Lord had caused his law to be written for them, but they did not care to know it, and would not obey it. People may appear mindful of their Maker by the temples they build, yet really they have forgotten him because they have cast off all fear; but no one has hardened his heart against God and prospered. So long as people despise the truths and commands of God’s word, and the practices of his worship, all observances and offerings, however costly, will be sin for them; for only those services are acceptable to God which are done according to his word, and through Jesus Christ.
